mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real auto sales and elevated federal funds rates indicate demand destruction in interest-sensitive sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no statistically significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) compared to either series alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and supply-side capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during post-shock recovery periods, or 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO growth (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 315.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 315.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic strength across labor and construction sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when either series enters a contraction phase, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Exceptionally high real auto sales signal peak consumer demand and potential inventory overhang, triggering a short-term mean reversion that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: High auto sales consistently predict positive 5-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, contradicting the mean reversion claim.
